description: This chart plots the statistical size distribution of dislocation cells ($D_{DC}$) in type 2 dislocation structures of austenitic Fe30Mn6.5Al0.3C low-density steel, measured from electron channeling contrast (ECC) images. The blue curve corresponds to the hydrogen-charged sample with 101 mass ppm hydrogen content, while the red curve corresponds to the uncharged sample with <5 mass ppm hydrogen content. The x-axis presents dislocation cell size in nm, and the y-axis presents the fraction of cells with the corresponding size. The average dislocation cell size is $220\pm10$ nm for the uncharged sample, and is refined to $192\pm10$ nm after hydrogen charging, indicating that hydrogen induces dislocation cell size refinement in the tested steel, which is consistent with previous observations in face-centered cubic metals. This figure supports the conclusion that hydrogen modifies deformation dislocation structures in this low-density steel.
